items.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt` **Service directory:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/` **Triggered by:** commit 1b854213504f49440f2dede6631cc3a35fe71571 (#57924) by `@ArcturusZhang` ## What Changed PR #57924 added `resource-name-constraint @`@clientOption`` support to the Azure management-plane C# emitter. As part of this work, a new reference page was added at `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/client-options.md` documenting two supported `@`@clientOption`` keys: - `resource-rbac-roles` — defines RBAC role definitions for provisioning - `resource-name-constraint` *(newly added)* — overrides resource name min/max length and character pattern constraints ## Gaps Found - The top-level `README.md` at `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/README.md` has no mention of `@`@clientOption``, `resource-name-constraint`, or `resource-rbac-roles`. There is no link to `client-options.md`, making the feature undiscoverable for spec authors reading the README. - The `docs/index.mdx` only links to `emitter.md` ("See documentation") and does not reference `client-options.md`. Spec authors navigating the published docs will not find the supported `@`@clientOption`` keys. - No "Supported decorators" or "Advanced customization" section exists in the README to guide spec authors who need to customize resource name constraints or RBAC roles.
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### File 1: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/README.md` **Edit** this file. After the existing emitter options section (the last option is `skip-api-version-override`), add a new section: ```markdown ## Supported `@`@clientOption`` keys The emitter recognizes additional metadata for resource code generation via the TCGC [`@`@clientOption``]((azure.github.io/redacted) decorator scoped to `"csharp"`. For the full reference with examples and generated output, see [Supported @`@clientOption` Keys](./docs/client-options.md). ### `resource-rbac-roles` Defines RBAC role definitions for a resource. Used by the provisioning generator to emit `BuiltInRole` structs and `CreateRoleAssignment` methods. ### `resource-name-constraint` Overrides resource name constraints (min length, max length, and valid character pattern). Useful when adding `@pattern`, `@minLength`, or `@maxLength` decorators directly to the spec would cause breaking changes in the generated swagger. ``` #### File 2: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/index.mdx` **Edit** this file. Change the existing `## Emitter usage` section's documentation link from the current: ```mdx ## Emitter usage [See documentation](./emitter.md) ``` to: ```mdx ## Emitter usage [See documentation](./emitter.md) ## Supported `@`@clientOption`` keys [See reference](./client-options.md) ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No test-backed snippets are required for this change. The content in `client-options.md` already contains TypeSpec and C# code examples. The README additions should be plain markdown prose with inline links — no `Update-Snippets.ps1` tooling is needed. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The `README.md` for this package is a tooling/generator README, not a client SDK README, so it does not need the standard Getting Started / Key Concepts / Examples / Troubleshooting / Next Steps / Contributing structure. However, verify that the new section: 1. Is placed at the end of the emitter options, before any `## Contributing` or closing section. 2. Uses correct relative link paths (e.g., `./docs/client-options.md`). 3. Does not duplicate content already present in `client-options.md` — the README should describe the feature briefly and link to the reference file. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order from the repository root: 1. `cd eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt && npm install` — ensure dependencies are installed 2. `npm run lint` — verify no lint errors in emitter or docs 3. `npm run prettier` — verify formatting There are no C# build or test steps required for this documentation-only change. The `Export-API.ps1` and `Update-Snippets.ps1` scripts are not applicable here (this is an emitter/generator package, not a C# client SDK).
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
items.title: [Azure Management Generator] Docs: Link and surface new @`@clientOption` reference page in README and docs index
